title: Argentinian Chicken Empanadas
description: A springy and herbal Argentinian dish featuring chicken empanadas with meringue nests and double-fry KFC style, perfect for a meal prep lunch
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of chicken breast in the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten eggs for a golden glaze.
Fold the dough in half over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with olive oil and sprinkle with paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Double-fry the empanadas by first baking them in the oven for 15 minutes, then frying them in hot oil until golden brown.
Drain the empanadas on paper towels and serve in meringue nests with a sprinkle of parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice.
